* **Notification Issues? EEPD-84712**  
  * HB for event  
    * If there, check archiver for ALRS (Motion Alert) tag (lqservices.html)  
      * If there, check VMLogs for alerts & accounts/ESN  
        * If there, if API doesn’t then no AEDN… OFF TO OBC  
* **Kernal bugs**  
  * journalctl \-k  
  * journalctl \-r \--grep=kernel  
* **LPR lqservices.html**  
  * [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/2943943106/Accessing+the+archiver+for+LPR+events](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/2943943106/Accessing+the+archiver+for+LPR+events)   
* **Permdocs?**  
  * Log in via VMS  
  * Get authkey (F12 \> Application \> authkey  
  * https://dproxy.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/dhash/node/v1/com.eencloud.dhash.cluster:\<cluster\>:/auth/\<authkey\>  
  * [https://dproxy.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/dhash/node/v1/com.eencloud.dhash.cluster:c007:/auth/d2d5e419bae6bf680016156553b7df2d](https://dproxy.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/dhash/node/v1/com.eencloud.dhash.cluster:c007:/auth/d2d5e419bae6bf680016156553b7df2d)  
* **Jobs**  
  * kubernetes.container\_name: "media-transcoder" AND (log.extra.jobId: "9e1905bb-8231-4c57-8116-bc81d618407c" OR 9e1905bb-8231-4c57-8116-bc81d618407c)  
  * [https://api.c026.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs/18a45944-6c63-4004-b33e-9616797bb86a](https://api.c026.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs/18a45944-6c63-4004-b33e-9616797bb86a)  
* **Delete Download?**  
  * Click on download \> copy call as cURL  
    * /api/v3.0/downloads/\<jobID\>:download  
    * /api/v3.0/downloads/0b02ee17-ef47-4fd1-8632-33bd383ad32a:download  
  * Go into postman \> copy as a new request \> change from GET to DELETE \> removed “download” at the end of the URL  
  * You’ll need the auth token and base URL  
    * Base URL should already be there with copying as curl  
* **Kube**  
  * kubectl config use-context \[cluster name\]  
    * kubectl config use-context nrt1p1  
  * kubectl get pods \--all-namespaces \-o wide | grep \[archiver name\]  
    * kubectl get pods \--all-namespaces \-o wide | grep a1514 | grep archiver  
    * kubectl get pods \-n default \-o wide | grep "136.242.84.32"  
  * kubectl cp archiver-\[whatever is in previous command\]:/opt/een/var/log/archiver/base/archiver.log \~/Archiver\_Logs/\[archiver name\].log  
    * kubectl cp archiver-pxwv6:/opt/een/var/log/archiver/base/archiver.log \~/Archiver\_Logs/a3495.log  
  * cat \~/Archiver\_logs/\[archiver name\].log | grep \[whatever you want e.x. redis, bridge ESN\]  
    * cat \~/Archiver\_logs/\<archiver\>.log | grep \<esn\>  
    * cat \~/Archiver\_logs/a3495.log | grep 1002ed35  
  * Jason  
    * kubectl node-shell a3205 \--context lon1p1  
    * cd /srv/archiver/var/lib/assets/100fdb20/a  
    * ls | grep ".etag" | wc \-l  
      * Are archivers in sync?  
    * /srv/archiver/var/lib/assets/100eb59a/a/2025/08/24 \# ls \-lh | grep \-v "etag"  
      * ls \-lh  
    * hexdump \-c 20251004130211.984\_h264\_high\_p0\_EVENT.flv | head \-50  
    * Corrupt flv?  
      * Vim the flv, look for ‘flv’ in white within first five or so characters  
    * Check if FLV with FLV tags, grep for FLV in file  
      * *a3538 /srv/archiver/var/lib/assets/100eb59a/a/2025/08/25 \#* grep FLV 20251201143742.000\_h264\_high.flv  
        * grep: 20250825215709.131\_h264\_high.flv: binary file matches  
* **Victorialogs**  
  * HelpDoc: [https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victorialogs/logsql/\#stream-filter](https://docs.victoriametrics.com/victorialogs/logsql/#stream-filter)  
  * kubernetes.container\_name:"media-service" cluster:"aus1p16" \_time:1h  
  * kubernetes.container\_name: "badger" | cluster: "aus1p16" | \_msg:"err creating tunnel with guid"  
  * kubernetes.container\_namespace:”\<namespace\>”  
    * apiv3  
    * qa-integration  
    * jobs-reports  
    * default  
    * videosearch-apis  
    *   
  * kubernetes.container\_name:”\<service\>”  
    * kubernetes.container\_name:”status-server”  
    * media-service  
    * alertid  
    * operator  
    * vms-core-api  
    * cameraendpoint  
    * status-server  
    * events3ingress  
    * herald  
    * registry  
    * videosearchworkers-inference  
    * webui-log-collector  
    * frontend-gui  
    * vms-core-pulsar-consumer  
    * gateway  
    * vmswebapp  
    * event-normalizer  
    * media-service  
    * ambrose  
      * Create vehicle lists  
    * oyez-consumer  
    * cm-auth  
    * virtualdevices-api  
    * cm-basurl-v3  
    * drivefs-notifier  
    * eventsv3alerts  
    * eventsv3api  
    * events3subscriptions  
    * eventsv3ingress  
    * jobs-api  
    * b2d2-pulsar-init  
    * herald & herald-consumer  
    * pos-ingester & pos  
    * reports-api  
    * user-details  
    * videosearch-apis  
    *   
  * (\_msg:\~\`10069d0c\`)  
  * kubernetes.container\_name:"media-service" cluster:"aus1p16" "1008de29" NOT RtspException  
  * kubernetes.container\_name:"media-service" cluster:"aus1p16" (\_msg:\~\`1008de29\` OR log.extra.camera\_id:"1008de29" OR log.extra.camera-id:"1008de29") \_time:\>2025-05-19T20:23Z  
  * State changed from PLAYING to STOPPED  
* **Webhook**  
  * kubernetes.pod\_labels.app.kubernetes.io/instance:="oyez-consumer" AND log.extra.success:="true" AND log.extra.action\_type:="webhook" AND account\_id:"00181761"  
* **Jobs**  
  * 1st command  
    * curl \-s "https://api.\<cluster\>.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs/\<taskID\>" \-H "X-EEN-Internal-API-Key: 127fbee9-0220-4ef4-88e9-a49057321a0c" | jq  
    * curl \-s "https://api.c022.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs/c33b4fae-8659-4053-87ba-c806fa7bc69b" \-H "X-EEN-Internal-API-Key: 127fbee9-0220-4ef4-88e9-a49057321a0c" | jq  
  * Sub jobs for export:  
    * curl \-s "https://api.c001.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs?userId=ca0603b4pageSize=500\&rootId=e1ba5d29-544b-4389-bf84-6586be85b93d" \-H "X-EEN-Internal-API-Key: 127fbee9-0220-4ef4-88e9-a49057321a0c" | jq  
    * curl \-s "https://api.c001.eagleeyenetworks.com/api/v3.0/jobs?userId=ca0603b4pageSize=500\&rootId=e1ba5d29-544b-4389-bf84-6586be85b93d" \-H "X-EEN-Internal-API-Key: 127fbee9-0220-4ef4-88e9-a49057321a0c" | jq  
* **Check settings on bridge**  
  * for i in /opt/een/var/bridge/esns/\*/settings;do echo $i | awk \-F/ '{print $7}';python \-m json.tool $i | grep \-i reten | sort;done  
* **Webfilter??**  
  * curl \-s [c006.bridge.eencloud.com](http://c006.bridge.eencloud.com)  
* **DNS**  
  * cat /etc/resolv.conf  
  * dig  
* **Custom Support file?**  
  * cat \<guid\>/configure/settings  
  * cat 39ade894-2df3-50b2-a205-58677f4790ec/configure/settings  
* **SSH into offline bridge, from 2nd offline bridge**  
  * Find bridges on network  
    * nmap \-oG \- \-p 33022 $(ip route | grep \-v default | grep \-E 'swan|eth1' | awk \-F ' \+' '{ print $1}') | grep open  
  * ssh root@10.20.3.29 \-p 33022  
  * ra\!3@KNS3pMi  
  * 192.168.10.1  
  * Bridge config  
    * ssh admin@172.16.0.241  
  * ssh root@192.168.1.98 \-p 33022  
    * Don’t need 33022 for plat1  
  * ssh \-p 33022 mfg@10.0.200.33  
    * ssh \-p 33022 mfg@10.0.200.33  
    * sudo su to go into host  
  * MFG password (as long as not on EEN network): een\<last5/6 backwards\>  
* **Check for SIGABRT/SIGTERM**  
  * docker logs bridge-bridge-1 |grep \-i sig  
  * docker logs bridge\_bridge\_1 |grep \-i sig  
* **Statuses**  
  * v3: camera \> bridge \> drops status to stream \>\> archiver picks up status \>\> status server listens for archiver publishing \>\> registry listens for status updates \>\> registry is query-able  
  * v1: stefani gets info from archivers (but if 1 archiver is down.. then bad statuses)  
  * Get status for all cams on account  
    * curl https://registry.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/Search?account\_id=\<account\>  
    * curl https://registry.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/Search?account\_id=00183526  
* **Fix Hold State for Cameras**  
  * connect to bridge host  
  * cd /opt/een/etc/bridge  
  * cp bridge.conf bridge\_local.conf  
  * vim bridge\_local.conf  
  *   
  * Scroll down to bridgevalidate=false  
  * change to  
  * bridge validate=true  
  * save the file  
  *   
  * restart bridge  
* **Going from stage to release?**  
  * vim /opt/een/data/docker-compose/bridge/docker-compose.yml && systemctl restart docker-compose@bridge  
  * Line needs to show: “image: c026.bridge.eencloud.com/bridge/bridge-guest:3.25.1”  
    * Save and exit, container will be restarted right after the new image is pulled  
* **Websocker for Previews**  
  * http://100fb653.a.plumv.com:28080/ws\_poll3.html  
  * { "cameras": { "100fb653": { "resource" : \[ "event","image"\] }}}  
* **Uptime for some logs**  
  * \[root@een-br406-127092 \~\]\# cat /proc/uptime  
    2344199.74 4825444.00  
  * \[root@een-br406-127092 \~\]\# dmesg | tail \-100 | grep \-i "gpu hang"  
    \[2339835.276735\] i915 0000:00:02.0: \[drm\] anpr\_detector\[1530821\] context reset due to GPU hang  
    \[2339835.284359\] i915 0000:00:02.0: \[drm\] GPU HANG: ecode 11:1:8ed9fff3, in anpr\_detector \[1530821\]  
    \[2340243.916687\] i915 0000:00:02.0: \[drm\] anpr\_detector\[1537385\] context reset due to GPU hang  
* **Dproxy**  
  * [https://dproxy.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/dhash/node/v1/com.eencloud.dhash.cluster:c001:/auth/b54974d004601f7b97e03f462f7dce7d](https://dproxy.test.eencloud.com/api/v2/dhash/node/v1/com.eencloud.dhash.cluster:c001:/auth/b54974d004601f7b97e03f462f7dce7d)  
  * Account number is hexadecimal  
  * Dproxy is decimal  
* **Docker Logs**  
  * sudo docker logs bridge-bridge-1 \--since "2025-12-22T11:00:00" \--until "2025-12-22T11:30:00"  
* **Raid**  
  * **Units**  
    * 520: 4 disks, raid5, 1 spare, 40tb (32 usable)  
    * 620: 4 disks, raid5, 1 spare, 64tb (48 usable)  
    * 820: 12 disks, raid6, 2 spare, 120tb (100 usable)  
  * bridge-diag  
  * cat /proc/mdstat  
  * findmnt /opt/een/data  
    * 520s should be /dev/nvme0n1p4 mntd on /opt/een/data  
  * df \-h /opt/een/data  
  * mdadm \--detail /dev/md127  
  * cat /proc/mdstat ; echo "" ; echo "Number of DISKs: $(fdisk \-l | grep \-c /dev/sd)" ;  fdisk \-l | grep /dev/sd  
  * smartctl \-a /dev/\<drive\>  
    * smartctl \-a /dev/sda  
    * Quicker (does all); for i in /dev/sd?; do smartctl \-a $i; done  
  *   
* CLOSE EOD  
  * [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-84632](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-84632)

—————————————————————————————————————————

To download footage:

login to account;

https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.flv?id=\<esn of the camera\>\&start\_timestamp=\<sett start time\>\&end\_timestamp=\<set end time\>  
\----- use this link in new tab, specify cameras esn, and time

https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.flv?id=100200a1\&start\_timestamp=20250424103400.000\&end\_timestamp=20250424103700.000

timestamp format:  
start:  20250424103400.000  
end:  20250424103700.000

OR  
Attempt to download the desired footage from the archiver directly.

http://{archiver}.eagleeyenetworks.com:28080/asset/play/video.flv?id={esn}\&start\_timestamp={start}\&end\_timestamp={end}\&options=coalesce

If you are unsure about what timestamps, you can manually review the available timestamps:  
http://{archiver}.eagleeyenetworks.com:28080/video.html?c={esn}

To have footage downloaded with timestamps:

https://login.eagleeyenetworks.com/asset/play/video.flv?id=\<camera ESN\>\&start\_timestamp=\<start time in UTC\>\&end\_timestamp=\<end time UTC\>\&osd=\<timezone\>

ARTEM:

* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-89009](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-89009)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88999](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88999)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88993](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88993)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88970](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88970)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88967](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88967)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-77413](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-77413)  
  * [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-87085](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-87085)  
* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-87077](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-87077)  
* 

TJ:

* [https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88724](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-88724)  
* 

[https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132247](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132247)

- Wanting to close ticket before even looking into everything??  
- ![][image1]  
- When asked about it, he said that Jayesh needs to do some work and give us info/updates…

[https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132280](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132280)

- Barely any work done and immediately wants to go to other dept for help, without us doing our due diligence  
- 

[https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132306](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EENS-132306)

- All the relevant information is in the ticket.. The timestamp of the footage needed… at this point, we replicate that exact timestamp.. If we get an error, we move it.  
- Instead of trying to understand why his footage was wrong, his first point of contention that my time conversion from UTC to US/Central was 1 hour wrong…  
- Advised that we need to make sure out replication steps are as close to 1:1 as possible  
  - Didn’t quite understand the need for 1:1 when he was able to replicate the issue with a bigger chunk of footage  
    - Conversation wasn’t going anywhere but in regards to footage, it matters to be 1:1 as possible because the download might be failing because part of the footage is corrupted  
- ![][image2]

[https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-89804](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-89804)

- Engineer mentioned that issue might be resolved, but we had a crash shortly after that comment and SWAT said that there were no further issues (without checking)

- Steve also saw, but Husain was upstairs on Jun 10, 10am. Asked him if he was upstairs talking to them about a jira ticket. He said he was talking to JJ about a ticket. I advised that we don’t need to be going to JJ to discuss a jira ticket and Husain then pivoted to coming up here because Brad told him to go look at the lab upstairs. Lab is downstairs; I told Husain that JJ isn’t the point of contact for the lab and that he needs to go discuss with Rafael. Husain questioned why I was questioning why he was upstairs talking to JJ. “You need to ask if I’m working? You think I’m up here just because?”

Discussion with Husain:

- Started off with a tirade full of expletives  
  - Advised that let’s be respectful and not cuss  
  - Said he already feels disrespected   
- Feels like I’m too defensive when I ask for more clarifying questions  
  - Today, he asked about a Compass link  
    - Advised that not many people have access to compass, Chris and I gave him information that should be relatable  
    - Also said “It will probably be no different than what Chris and I gave you.” was unnecessary in regards to me commenting on compass  
- Said he was disrespected when I asked him why he was near JJ & Aroofa today and if they were working on a ticket  
  - Husain needed information on the support lab, I advised that JJ isn’t the best poc but he should go to Rafa for any questions about qa lab  
    - Asked Husain how man AHM we’ve been to where Rafa has been discussed as the guy all things lab  
- I sent a zulip message in the swat chat today that we’ve got to be more mindful when going up to support for help  
  - We shouldn’t be going to support for help in regards to how an archiver works and why it’s broke, etc  
  - But it’s fine if we need more information from them in regards to a ticket (I do it all the time)  
- Another point of discussion was that he doesn’t ‘want to be taught how to think”  
  - I asked him if he would rather just have the straight answer (yes/no/maybe/etc) instead of learning how/why the answer is the answer  
    - He admited it only wants the straight answer, doesn’t want to know the why as it essentially is ‘being taught how to think’  
  - I believe this is a big point of contention and leaves me to think that this might not work out…  
    - I advised him that I was this same way as a PL. I try and make the other person think and guide them with finding the answer as opposed to giving the answer  
      - How can someone retain info if they’re always given the answer and don’t have to do any critical thinking ?  
- Discussed that when My was asked to no longer visit , that it was asked for us get some glue on swat members  
  - Advised that there was an instance/precedence last week where Husain was up there for 40 minutes with no laptop and just talking to Aroofa  
    - He says he was talking to her and learning  
      - Didn’t debate that  
- Husain felt disrespected by me tagging everyone involved in the jira with an update about the issue not being resolved and still need investigating   
- Aroofa has expressed complaints to Support management that she is being bothered too much  
- Husain started to alude that he has already talked to you and seemed like he was trying to pry me asking what was discussed  
  - I told him I figured you guys talked but nothing was mentioned about him by name to me  
    - Mentioned that we did discuss teaching styles and similar things like that  
  - I also told him that I don’t need to know what y’all talked about and there’s an entire point of y’alls confidentiality that you haven’t broke  
    - But told Husain he’s more than welcome to break that, but I’m not asking for it  
- Husain mentioned it seems like I’m hoping for him to fail and doesn’t seem like I’m trying to help  
  - I mentioned that me asking him about tickets is me trying to help  
    - If I wanted him to fail, I don’t imagine I would be asking about the tickets and just let them rot no action taken

project \= "Eagle Eye Product Development" OR project \= Support AND "Original Assignee" IN ("Christopher Luther" , "Adam Garcia" , "David Pandy" , "Husain Alshaikahmed")

- What issues are we observing? Whether it’s Support, Engineering, etc.?  
- What is working well? Whether it’s Support, Engineering, etc.?  
- We are learning from other depts and meeting with other engineers. What are we learning? How are we using this information to aid ourselves in our roles?  
- Any other questions/concerns?

[https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-83044](https://eagleeyenetworks.atlassian.net/browse/EEPD-83044)
